title: Decarbonization Plan
description: Schema for the decarb report type — full-engagement decarbonization plan: validated building model, calibrated baseline, emissions trajectory, phased measure roadmap, and a direct-cap value-creation waterfall + annual cashflow per plan (Audette investment models, recipe 8). CLIENT-RENDER template: the server injects this object into <script id="report-data"> and templates/decarb/layout-agent.html renders every section and chart from it (fill_report path, same as RSRA). The Measure Roadmap renders from economics.measures[]; the waterfall, cashflow, and plan comparison render from economics.plans[].

property.description: Core property identification and physical attributes. Renders the cover header and meta bar.

address.description: Full street address including city, state.

units.description: Unit count. Required for multifamily/senior-living/student-housing; omit for CRE.

gfa_sqft.description: Gross floor area in square feet. Used for CRE; drives the meta-bar Size line when units is absent.

gfa_m2.description: Gross floor area in m². Optional; supports kWh·m⁻² derivations.

prepared_by.description: Human preparer who produced this plan. Default 'Christopher Naismith, Soapbox'. Never an AI persona (e.g. not 'Aris' or 'Soapbox AI').

report_date.description: ISO 8601 date the report was generated.

executive_summary.description: Top-line summary. Either a plain string (rendered as the narrative) or an object with a headline + narrative.

headline.description: One-line bold conclusion (e.g. 'Deep electrification is CRREM-aligned and value-accretive at exit').

narrative.description: One to three sentences naming the recommended plan, the net value creation, and the primary decision driver.

dashboard.description: Decision Dashboard — the hero section rendered right after the doc header. Every field is nullable/omittable: a missing field hides its tile. If this object is ABSENT entirely, the template falls back to the legacy executive-summary row, so old payloads keep working. All figures must come from engines/Audette models — never LLM-computed.

title.description: Dashboard headline. Defaults to 'Fund decision at a glance' when omitted.

compliance.description: Compliance status chip (BPS / primary regulatory driver, with-plan).

status.description: Chip color: green = compliant with plan, amber = marginal/conditional, red = non-compliant.

label.description: Chip text (e.g. 'Columbus BPS: compliant through 2030 (with plan)').

net_value_creation.description: Hero tile: net value creation at exit in USD (selected plan's waterfall net).

irr.description: Hero tile: unlevered incremental IRR as a decimal (e.g. 0.09 for 9%).

irr_hurdle.description: IRR hurdle as a decimal; shown as the IRR tile's comparison sub-line and in the chip caption.

capital_ask.description: Tile: incremental capital ask in USD (positive number).

ghgi_change_pct.description: Tile: GHGI change by the target year as a signed decimal (NEGATIVE for reductions, e.g. -0.46 for −46%).

ghgi_target_year.description: Year the ghgi_change_pct is measured at (e.g. 2030).

downside_avoided.description: Tile: PV of avoided BPS fines ONLY (actual jurisdiction fine avoidance) in USD. CRREM stranding is NOT monetized — never add a stranding-risk dollar value. 0 (or omit the tile) if the asset faces no actual fines.

cf_positive_year.description: Tile: first year annual unlevered incremental cashflow turns positive.

recommendation.description: The recommendation, as a SHORT digestible summary — MAXIMUM 3 sentences. State the recommended plan, the single most important reason, and the key trade-off/condition. NOT a wall of text, NOT a full plan description (the per-plan detail lives in economics.plans[].summary_points). E.g. 'Fund Plan 2 — the CRREM-aligned electrification roadmap. It is the only path that keeps the asset non-stranded through a 2036 exit and unlocks green-financing pricing. The trade-off is a lower near-term IRR that Plan 1's capital-light bundle would otherwise maximize.'

baseline.description: Validated building model + calibrated baseline. All displayed metrics rendered at 2 significant figures; energy in kWh and kWh·m⁻².

model_validation.description: One line on how the building model was validated (e.g. 'Geometry reconciled against ALTA/PCA; equipment from on-site survey 2026-05').

calibrated_kwh.description: Calibrated annual site energy in kWh/yr.

fuel_profile.description: Primary energy/fuel profile (e.g. 'Dual-fuel — gas heat + DHW, electric cooling').

utility_structure.description: Who pays utilities and how metered.

calibration_basis.description: Basis for calibration (e.g. '24 months ESPM-linked actuals; zero-variance GFA-weighted building allocation').

targets.description: Carbon pathway targets and trajectory. Feeds the Emissions Trajectory line chart.

crrem_stranding_year.description: Year the planned trajectory crosses (strands against) the CRREM 1.5°C pathway. Optional; shown as an annotated dot + chart footnote. When omitted and crrem_pathway is present, the template computes it as the first year planned > pathway (tracks planned_1 in a two-path report).

plan1_label.description: Optional legend label for the planned_1 (capital-light) trajectory line, e.g. 'Plan 1 — Capital-Light'.

plan2_label.description: Optional legend label for the planned_2 (deep-decarbonization) trajectory line, e.g. 'Plan 2 — Deep Decarbonization'.

crrem_pathway.description: The ONLY place a CRREM pathway may appear. This MUST be the REAL CRREM decarbonization pathway for the asset's country/region/property type, from the crrem MCP server (get_pathway) — NEVER fabricated, interpolated, or hand-entered. There is NO inline per-year CRREM field anymore (the legacy trajectory[].crrem_target was removed); populate this array from get_pathway and set crrem_meta so the server-side gate can re-fetch and verify it. A render with a hand-built CRREM curve (or with values that don't match get_pathway within tolerance) is BLOCKED. Drawn as a distinct dashed curve ALONGSIDE the stepped BPS target line: BPS drives fines, CRREM drives stranding. Units must be kgCO₂e·m⁻²·yr⁻¹ to match the trajectory series (convert if the asset's GHGI is tracked per-ft²).

carbon_kgco2_m2yr.description: CRREM pathway carbon intensity in kgCO₂/m²/yr.

crrem_meta.description: Provenance of the CRREM pathway — rendered as a chart footnote AND used by the server gate to re-fetch and verify the curve. CRITICAL: set these to the EXACT values the get_pathway RESPONSE echoes back (it returns the canonical propertyType/region it resolved to), NOT a hand-written display label. If you store a loose label the gate's re-fetch can't resolve, the render is BLOCKED as unverifiable.

country.description: Country code exactly as passed to get_pathway (e.g. 'US').

region.description: Canonical CRREM region as echoed by the get_pathway response (e.g. 'RMPAc_Cool_5A/5B') — not a paraphrase.

property_type.description: Canonical CRREM property type as echoed by the get_pathway response (e.g. 'large_multi-family_-_high_rise_(>20_units)') — not a display label.

trajectory.description: Per-year carbon-intensity points (kgCO₂e·m⁻²·yr⁻¹) for BAU, the planned trajectory, and the CRREM 1.5°C target. Minimum 2 points to render a line.

bau.description: Business-as-usual carbon intensity (kgCO₂e·m⁻²). Use the Audette-modeled baseline carbon curve where available (grid-factor drift included) — never a fabricated flat line.

planned.description: Planned/selected-plan carbon intensity (kgCO₂e·m⁻²). Use for a SINGLE-plan report. For a two-path report use planned_1 + planned_2 instead so both curves render.

planned_1.description: Plan 1 (capital-light) carbon intensity (kgCO₂e·m⁻²) — drawn as a green line. Use with planned_2 to contrast both strategic paths on the chart.

planned_2.description: Plan 2 (deep-decarbonization) carbon intensity (kgCO₂e·m⁻²) — drawn as a teal line; should stay under the CRREM curve (non-stranded).

bps_target.description: BPS / regulatory compliance target intensity for this year (kgCO₂e·m⁻²). Drawn as a STEPPED navy dashed line — the fines driver, distinct from the CRREM stranding pathway. Provide only for years where a target is defined.

bps_label.description: Legend/footnote label for the carbon bps_target line, e.g. 'Seattle BEPS (GHGI target)'. Defaults to 'BPS compliance target' when omitted.

bps_source.description: Provenance citation for the bps_target values (jurisdiction + standard + table/rule + URL). Required whenever any trajectory point sets bps_target. Rendered as a chart footnote; NOT server-verified.

eui_compliance.description: Energy-based BPS standards (e.g. WA Clean Buildings Act / WSCBA) shown as a compact compliance panel. Omit or empty to hide the panel. Each entry is one standard applicable to the asset's jurisdiction.

standard.description: Standard name, e.g. 'WA Clean Buildings Act (WSCBA)'.

unit.description: EUI display unit, e.g. 'kBtu/sf/yr' or 'kWh/m2/yr'. building_eui and target_eui must be in this unit.

target_eui.description: The standard's EUI target (EUIt) for this property type in `unit`.

projected_eui.description: The building's projected EUI AFTER the plan's measures, in `unit` (the 'after'). Optional; when present, the panel shows current → target → after so the reader sees the plan clear the line.

annual_fine_avoided.description: Stabilized annual BPS fine avoided (USD/yr) once the plan brings the building into compliance — the recurring penalty eliminated. Owner-borne (fines are never rebilled to tenants). Omit if no fine regime applies.

fine_schedule.description: Annual fine ($/yr) over time, driving the fine-avoidance area chart: the BAU (no-action) fine vs the with-plan fine, which drops to $0 once the plan brings EUI under target. Omit to fall back to the compact current-vs-target bar. Every dollar figure must trace to the fine formula in `source` — never fabricate.

bau_fine.description: Annual fine (USD/yr) with no action — building EUI stays above target as the target tightens (phased). Stepped up at each compliance milestone.

plan_fine.description: Annual fine (USD/yr) with the plan in place — trends to 0 in the year the plan's projected EUI drops under the then-current target.

source.description: Provenance citation (WAC/rule/table + URL). Required — never fabricate.

economics.description: Direct-cap value-creation economics (recipe 8). Carries the exit assumptions, the measure list (renders the Measure Roadmap), and one or more plans each with a waterfall + annual cashflow.

exit_cap.description: Exit cap rate as a decimal (e.g. 0.0575 for 5.75%). Used to capitalize recurring NOI changes.

bps_note.description: Optional override for the note explaining a '—' BPS fine-avoidance column (shown when no plan models a fine regime). Defaults to a generic 'no mandatory BPS penalty regime in this jurisdiction' explanation.

selected_plan.description: The `name` of the recommended plan. Visually emphasized in the waterfall; drives which plan's cashflow is shown.

measures.description: All modeled measures. Renders the Measure Roadmap table (measure, type, install year, incremental cost, savings). Only the incremental cost over like-for-like hits capex/IRR.

name.description: Measure name (e.g. 'Air-source heat pump conversion — 2 of 4 plants').

type.description: Measure family (e.g. 'Electrification', 'Add-on', 'Replacement-at-RUL', 'Solar PV'). Used as the roadmap group header.

plan.description: Which plan this measure belongs to — renders the 'Plan' tag. Use 'Both plans', 'Plan 1 only', or 'Plan 2 only' (the template shortens to Both / Plan 1 / Plan 2).

install_year.description: Staged install year (to equipment RUL / capital event). Renders the 'Year' column.

like_for_like_cost.description: Like-for-like replacement cost in USD (0 for pure add-ons). Renders 'Like-for-Like Cost'.

incremental_cost.description: Incremental cost over like-for-like (= total − like_for_like). Renders 'Incremental Cost'; drives Net Total Cost and Y1 RoC.

incentives.description: Rebates/incentives applied to THIS measure in USD (positive). Renders 'Incentives'; Net Total Cost = incremental_cost − incentives.

emissions_savings_tco2e.description: Annual emissions reduction from this measure in tCO₂e/yr (engine-computed from gas/elec deltas × emission factors — never LLM-computed). Renders 'Emissions Savings (tCO₂e)'.

landlord_utility_savings.description: Stabilized annual OWNER/LANDLORD-SHARE utility $ savings the owner actually captures (per the end-use payer). Renders 'Landlord Utility Savings' and drives Y1 RoC. (Alias: annual_owner_savings — landlord_utility_savings wins if both present.) From the engine, never LLM-computed.

tenant_utility_savings.description: Stabilized annual TENANT-SHARE utility $ savings (does NOT accrue to the owner; shown separately, never merged with landlord savings and never capitalized into the owner value bridge). Renders 'Tenant Utility Savings'.

ancillary_revenue.description: Stabilized annual ancillary revenue from this measure (EV charging, PJM DR, solar export) in USD/yr, risk-adjusted. Renders 'Ancillary Revenue' and is included in the Y1 RoC numerator.

annual_owner_savings.description: DEPRECATED alias for landlord_utility_savings (kept for back-compat). Prefer landlord_utility_savings.

elec_reduction_kwh.description: Annual electricity reduction in kWh. NEGATIVE for electrification measures (electricity rises).

plans.description: Scenario bundles (e.g. Plan 1 near-term positive-IRR set, Plan 2 deep electrification). Each renders its own value-creation waterfall + a row in the Plan Comparison; the selected plan's cashflow is shown.

summary_points.description: 3-5 short plain-language bullets describing this plan for readability — what it deploys, the headline IRR/value, the key sensitivity or trade-off. Rendered as a bulleted list under the plan heading. PREFER this over cramming the plan narrative into executive_summary as a paragraph wall.

irr_incremental.description: IRR on incremental spend as a decimal (e.g. 0.25 for 25%).

ghgi_reduction_pct.description: Plan's GHGI reduction by the target year as a positive decimal (e.g. 0.58 for a 58% reduction). Nullable; shown in the Scenario Comparison strip.

compliant.description: Whether this plan achieves BPS compliance. Nullable; shown as ✓ Compliant / At risk in the Scenario Comparison strip.

waterfall.description: The value-creation-bridge components plus the net total. incremental_capex and capitalized_subscription are stored NEGATIVE (they pull the bridge down); all additive components are positive. net_value_creation should equal the sum of the components (incremental_capex + capitalized_subscription + incentives + capitalized_utility_savings + capitalized_ancillary_revenue + pv_bps_fine_avoidance). baseline_capex is context-only (NOT summed into the bridge).

incremental_capex.description: Σ incremental cost over like-for-like across the plan's measures. NEGATIVE (decrement).

baseline_capex.description: Σ like-for-like (baseline) replacement cost that would be spent anyway across the plan's measures — the spend the incremental sits on top of. POSITIVE. Context/disclosure only: shown in the scenario comparison table, NOT summed into net_value_creation.

capitalized_subscription.description: Capitalized impact of any subscription-model measure's recurring FEE (e.g. Parity RCx) = stabilized annual subscription cost ÷ exit cap. NEGATIVE (a value drag). The savings the subscription enables flow through capitalized_utility_savings (gross); this is the separate, transparent cost line. 0 if no subscription measures.

capitalized_utility_savings.description: Stabilized annual owner-share utility $ savings ÷ exit cap. Positive. For subscription measures this is the GROSS savings (before the subscription fee, which is captured separately in capitalized_subscription).

capitalized_ancillary_revenue.description: Stabilized annual solar/EV ancillary revenue ÷ exit cap. Positive.

pv_bps_fine_avoidance.description: Discounted present value of the avoided-fine stream (a finite, scheduled penalty — PV, never capitalized). Positive. Set to NULL (omit — do NOT use 0) when the jurisdiction has no mandatory BPS penalty regime: there is no downside to avoid, and the report then shows '—' with an explanatory note rather than a misleading $0 'avoided' line.

net_value_creation.description: Impact on asset value at exit = incremental_capex + capitalized_subscription + incentives + capitalized_utility_savings + capitalized_ancillary_revenue + pv_bps_fine_avoidance (treat null fine-avoidance as 0 in this sum).

cashflow.description: Annual cashflow underneath the waterfall. NOI Impact = Revenue + Utility Savings + BPS Fine Avoidance; Unlevered Incremental Cashflow = NOI Impact − Incremental CapEx + Incentives; Asset Value Impact booked in the exit year.

revenue.description: Incremental ancillary revenue (e.g. solar/EV) in USD.

like_for_like_capex.description: Like-for-like capex in USD (informational; not in the incremental IRR).

incremental_capex.description: Incremental capex in USD (positive spend in the install year).

bps_fine_avoidance.description: BPS fine avoided that year in USD.

noi_impact.description: Revenue + Utility Savings + BPS Fine Avoidance.

asset_value_impact.description: Capitalized NOI uplift booked at exit (nonzero only in the exit year).

thesis.description: One-line plain-language thesis for this plan (what it does + the headline financial/compliance outcome). Rendered as a separated bulleted block in the Recommendation. Keep to one sentence; summary_points[] carries the detail.

bps_penalty_per_tco2e.description: BPS/BERDO/BEUDO penalty rate in $ per tCO2e over the compliance limit (e.g. 234 for Cambridge BEUDO 2026-2030). REQUIRED whenever any plan books bps_fine_avoidance. The render gate recomputes every year's fine avoidance from targets.trajectory (BAU/plan/bps_target kgCO2e/m2) x this rate x property.gfa_m2 and BLOCKS on divergence — so every fine dollar must derive from the trajectory, never hand-entered.

data_quality.description: Client-facing data-quality summary and per-input confidence dots. EXTERNAL DELIVERABLE: no internal-process language (no 'Gate 1/2', 'adjudicated', 'verifier', 'Confirmed finding <id>', 'phase', 'workflow'). Do NOT include the internal reconciliation ledger.

summary.description: One-line client-facing confidence statement and basis (e.g. 'Baseline built from 12 months of utility bills and an on-site equipment survey.'). Also populates the footer confidence note. No internal-process terms.

items.description: Per-input data-quality flags rendered as colored-dot bullets. Client-facing text only — cite the source ('AEI energy audit 475250-EA1'), never the internal step ('Locked at Gate 1').

text.description: The client-facing data-quality note. No internal-process terms.

methodology.description: Methodology note — provenance statement (e.g. 'Every reported number traces to an engine output or cited source; no figure is LLM-computed.').

sources.description: Citations for regulatory, incentive, and market claims. Rendered in a collapsible Sources list.

label.description: Short description of what this source provided.

url.description: Full URL (http/https only; validated before rendering).

value_cited.description: The specific data point taken from this source.
